reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real transformers.js token-classification in a dedicated module Worker (models/pos-tagging/worker.js): loadPipeline({task:"token-classification", model:"Xenova/french-camembert-postag-model", backend:"wasm", dtype:"q8"}) then pipe(text) for per-sub-token {entity,score,index,word}. CamemBERT is SentencePiece, so the worker MERGES sub-word pieces back into whole words (▁-boundary + a monotonic char-cursor walk over the ORIGINAL text), takes the word-initial tag, pools the score, and maps each word to its char span for inline highlighting. WASM demo (no WebGPU), runs everywhere. Conformance calls-real-inference (source) + loads-clean tested-pass; io-shape-semantic manual-evidenced. Latency/tags NOT fabricated in this backfill.
rubric.notes: Both the raw sub-tokens AND the merged words are returned so the page can show the genuine token->word reconstruction, not a simplification.

rubric.evidence: Expected output is per-token {entity(FTB tag), score, index, word}; the worker post-processes to per-WORD {tag, group, score, surface, start, end}. FTB tags (NC, NPP, V, ADJ, DET, P, CLS...) fold into 11 universal groups via groupOf() in pos.js. The page highlights each word inline in the original text coloured by group + shows the raw sub-tokens. Shape matches the token-classification pipeline contract.
rubric.notes: The char-span mapping makes inline highlighting land exactly on the original text (the pipeline returns no offsets).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/french-camembert-postag-model, linked) as ONNX of gilf/french-camembert-postag-model (linked), task (token-classification), the 30 FTB tags -> 11 groups, architecture (CamemBERT, RoBERTa-based SentencePiece ~110M), language (French — honestly noted as the closest working ONNX POS tagger), quantisation (q8), backend (WebAssembly, no WebGPU), download (~113 MB), license MIT. Matches worker.js. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation + declares-backend tested-pass.

Loads via shared createModelLoader (modelId:"Xenova/french-camembert-postag-model", dtype:"q8", sizeMB:113, requiresWebGPU:false). Valid cached model auto-initialises with no Load click; Download/Re-download/Update + clear-cache otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source) tested-pass; auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on the repo so the ~113 MB cache is tracked for the no-silent-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence:
Status is

(runStatus); the output region is aria-live; the textarea starts disabled and enables onReady. On failure the worker posts {type:error} surfaced on the page. Conformance live-status-region + errors-on-the-page tested-pass; runs-without-webgpu-fallback asserted; honest-progress-retry-offline manual.

Every control is real + keyboard-operable: the French text textarea (default 'Le vieux chat noir dort tranquillement sur le canapé du salon.'), the tag action, and a legend of the universal groups. Editing the text re-tags it against the real model.
No hardcoded tags: every word's part-of-speech + score come from the real pipe(text) call and the deterministic sub-word merge. Conformance no-fake-output manual-evidenced.

Status + output use role=status / aria-live; the French input carries the French text (a lang=fr on the input/output would further aid correct pronunciation of the highlighted words); buttons have text names (buttons-have-names tested-pass); no content images (images-have-alt vacuously pass); light+dark WCAG-AA. Group colours are backed by a text tag label per word, not colour alone.
rubric.notes: Minor: adding lang=fr to the French text region would improve screen-reader pronunciation of the tagged words.

rubric.evidence: Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are the tested assertions; the input + inline-highlighted output + legend wrap fluidly. parity-both-classes manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: WASM demo — fully usable on mobile; ~113 MB download surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Tagging runs in the dedicated module Worker; the sub-word merge + char-cursor walk are O(tokens) and also run off-thread; the main thread only renders the highlighted words. tagMany loops off-thread. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source: new Worker) tested-pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low run heavy work off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread and break up long tasks so the browser can service input and repaint; workers + yielding keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token-classification + the SentencePiece sub-word merge / char-span mapping run in a dedicated module Worker; the page thread only renders the highlighted words.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker(type:module) + off-thread mergeWords; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, accessible names for controls, loading state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; semantic HTML + visible focus; mark language for correct shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live status + aria-live output; each word carries a text tag label (not colour alone); accessible action names.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow using flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — input, inline-highlighted output and the group legend wrap fluidly; no horizontal overflow at mobile.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible form controls: range slider and text input labelling, keyboard operable
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Associate every input with a label, keep controls keyboard-operable, and reflect state changes accessibly.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the French text textarea is a labelled native control that re-tags on edit; the group legend documents what each colour/label means.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: native <textarea> re-runs tagging; legend maps groups to labels

openQuestions: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached are manual-evidenced — a headless WASM run could capture real per-word tags + latency to flip them.

The tagger is FRENCH (the closest working ONNX POS model) — is the language limitation prominent enough that an English-speaking visitor isn't surprised, and is an English ONNX POS export worth re-checking upstream?

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a real headless WASM tagging run (per-word FTB tags + latency) to satisfy io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached.
Add lang=fr to the French text region for correct screen-reader pronunciation of tagged words.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique for pos-tagging (Xenova/french-camembert-postag-model, ONNX of gilf/french-camembert-postag-model, token-classification, q8, WASM — runs everywhere, ~113 MB, MIT). A genuine FRENCH POS tagger (FTB tag set -> 11 universal groups): CamemBERT token-classification in a dedicated module Worker, with an honest SentencePiece sub-word->word merge and a char-cursor span mapping so inline highlighting lands exactly on the original text; both raw sub-tokens and merged words are shown. No WebGPU needed. French-only limitation stated honestly. No fabricated tags; latency NOT invented in this backfill. guidanceConsulted: break-up-long-tasks, accessibility, css-layout, forms. Weakness flagged: French-only; French text region lacks lang=fr; io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
